briefing.opening: Good morning/afternoon, everyone. We will now begin the climate briefing for March 6, 2026.

sections.title: 🔴 Accelerating Global Warming and Deepening Complex Climate Disasters
sections.content: According to domestic and international research, the rate of global warming has sharply accelerated since 2015, nearly doubling [2, 40, 43, 50, 57, 61]. Notably, 'complex disasters,' where heatwaves and droughts occur simultaneously, have surged eightfold domestically since the 2000s, leading to a vicious cycle where the atmosphere and land mutually warm each other [1, 3, 28, 58]. This accelerated warming is causing unexpected climate changes, such as earlier springs and increased snowfall in Japan [34, 36]. Scientists are continuing their research to unravel the mystery behind this acceleration of global warming [33].

sections.title: ⚠️ Direct Socio-Economic Impacts and Increased Vulnerability to Climate Change
sections.content: Accelerating climate change is having severe socio-economic impacts in various regions worldwide. Lobster catches in Maine, USA, have once again decreased due to high costs and the impacts of climate change [30, 44, 51, 54], and fishermen in Greenland are also facing threats to their livelihoods due to climate change [42]. Youth in California are appealing to the appellate court to examine the impacts of climate change on their lives, representing the concerns of future generations [29, 48]. Meanwhile, concerns have been raised that Europe's climate change agenda is derailing, along with warnings that modern buildings are at risk of being inadequately prepared for climate change [49, 55].

sections.title: 🌱 Domestic and International Efforts for Renewable Energy Expansion and Carbon Neutrality Implementation
sections.content: Amidst the climate change crisis, positive domestic and international movements to expand renewable energy are active. Chungcheongnam-do Province is attracting 1 trillion won in foreign investment from global renewable energy companies, promoting the creation of offshore wind farms and establishing itself as a renewable energy hub [4, 13, 18, 19, 20, 21, 27], and Yeonggwang County has declared its vision to become an 'Energy Basic Income City,' sharing renewable energy profits with its residents [12, 15, 17, 24, 25]. Various local governments in Korea are continuing their community efforts, including signing business agreements for carbon neutrality implementation, resolving five key tasks, and operating a carbon neutrality points system for vehicles [5, 6, 7, 8, 10]. The government is also accelerating the expansion of renewable energy supply by holding public-private discussions on reducing solar power generation costs and strengthening industrial competitiveness [22, 23, 26]. Internationally, various efforts are gaining attention, such as events encouraging climate change adaptation, research on new coffee tree varieties, and the allocation of response funds [31, 45, 53].

sections.title: 🌍 Policy Discussions and Trends in Public Awareness Enhancement for Climate Change Response
sections.content: Policy discussions on climate change and activities to enhance public awareness are also continuing. China is setting strategic directions for climate change through its 15th 'Five-Year Plan' [32, 59, 60], and California is advancing a statewide water conservation plan to combat climate change [37]. Meanwhile, research findings have been released suggesting that climate change will not have a fatal impact on electric vehicle batteries [39], and another study indicated that reliable solar power generation requires a larger land area than previously thought [47]. Lectures on climate change, glaciers, and polar bears are scheduled at the Juneau Library [38], and efforts to increase public understanding and empathy are continuing in various ways, including the production of documentaries and animated comedies addressing climate change [52, 56].

briefing.closing: As confirmed in today's briefing, the acceleration of global warming and the increase in complex disasters are sending a serious warning to humanity. However, at the same time, bold investments and policy efforts to expand renewable energy are actively underway both domestically and internationally, igniting a spark of hope. Addressing climate change is everyone's responsibility and an opportunity, requiring continuous attention and action. This concludes the climate briefing for March 6, 2026.

articles.title: Since the 2000s, 'Compound Disasters' of Heatwaves and Droughts Surge… 'Vicious Cycle' of Atmosphere and Ground Heating Each Other
articles.original_title: 2000년대 들어 폭염-가뭄 ‘복합재해’ 급증···대기와 지면 서로 데우는 ‘악순환’
articles.description: On August 31 last year, the bottom of Obong Reservoir in Seongsan-myeon, Gangneung-si, Gangwon Province, was dry. A domestic study has revealed a sharp increase in compound disasters, where heatwaves and droughts occur consecutively, since the early 2000s. Analysis suggests that a 'vicious cycle' is intensifying, where heatwaves, which once ended as simple hot weather, now lead to severe droughts that dry out the land, escalating into powerful compound disasters.

summary.phenomenon: Since the early 2000s, compound disasters involving consecutive heatwaves and droughts have sharply increased in South Korea. On August 31 last year, the bottom of Obong Reservoir in Seongsan-myeon, Gangneung-si, Gangwon Province, was dry.
summary.cause: Analysis suggests that a vicious cycle is intensifying, where heatwaves, which once ended as simple hot weather, now lead to severe droughts that dry out the land, causing the atmosphere and ground to heat each other.

articles.original_description: 지난해 8월31일 강원도 강릉시 성산면 오봉저수지 바닥이 말라 있다. 정효진 기자2000년대 초반 이후 폭염과 가뭄이 연달아 발생하는 복합재해가 급증했다는 국내 연구 결과가 나왔다. 과거에는 단순한 더위로 끝났던 폭염이 이제는 땅을 바짝 말리는 가뭄으로 이어지며 강력한 복합재해로 번지는 ‘악순환의 고리’가 강화되고 있다는 분석이다.7일 김용준 한양대 해양융···
